As holding periods extend, the stock price goes ex-dividend (specifically the May and August events), knocking absolute nominal price down over time unless matched by considerable enterprise value growth, producing a material headwind against the stock remaining above the April 13 threshold. 3. As instructed, our probabilities regress smoothly toward 0.5 over later horizons to account for the increasing unpredictability of pure market noise overcoming these systematic factors.
